text_original: Qian (represents) what is great and originating, penetrating, advantageous, correct and firm.
text_normalized: Qian (represents) what is great and originating, penetrating, advantageous, correct and firm.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=Judgment.

text_original: In the first (or lowest) NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon lying hid (in the deep). It is not the time for active doing.
text_normalized: In the first (or lowest) NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon lying hid (in the deep). It is not the time for active doing.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=first.

text_original: In the second NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon appearing in the field. It will be advantageous to meet with the great man.
text_normalized: In the second NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon appearing in the field. It will be advantageous to meet with the great man.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=second.

text_original: In the fourth NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as the dragon looking) as if he were leaping up, but still in the deep. There will be no mistake.
text_normalized: In the fourth NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as the dragon looking) as if he were leaping up, but still in the deep. There will be no mistake.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fourth.

text_original: In the fifth NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon on the wing in the sky. It will be advantageous to meet with the great man
text_normalized: In the fifth NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon on the wing in the sky. It will be advantageous to meet with the great man
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fifth.

text_original: In the sixth (or topmost) NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon exceeding the proper limits. There will be occasion for repentance.
text_normalized: In the sixth (or topmost) NINE, undivided, (we see its subject as) the dragon exceeding the proper limits. There will be occasion for repentance.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=top.

text_original: (The lines of this hexagram are all strong and undivided, as appears from) the use of the number NINE. If the host of dragons (thus) appearing were to divest themselves of their heads, there would be good fortune.
text_normalized: (The lines of this hexagram are all strong and undivided, as appears from) the use of the number NINE. If the host of dragons (thus) appearing were to divest themselves of their heads, there would be good fortune.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=use.

text_original: In the third NINE, there is a twofold symbol of) strength, but (the position) is not central. (Its occupant) is not in heaven above, nor is he in the field beneath. Therefore there must be active vigilance and cautious apprehension as the time requires; and. though (the position be) perilous, there will be no mistake.
text_normalized: In the third NINE, there is a twofold symbol of) strength, but (the position) is not central. (Its occupant) is not in heaven above, nor is he in the field beneath. Therefore there must be active vigilance and cautious apprehension as the time requires; and. though (the position be) perilous, there will be no mistake.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=third.
